annotations.anchor_quote: Godsends blind obedience:
annotations.comment: Misphrased header 'Godsends blind obedience' obscures stance; rephrase to 'ends blind obedience' for clear thesis alignment.

annotations.anchor_quote: It is an undeniable reality that by enlarging the female mind with education, there will be end to blind obedience, yet there are Various reasons which inhibit the process; consequently women across the globe suffer detrimental impacts.
annotations.comment: Introduction restates topic without explicit thesis or argument overview; add concise thesis previewing structure and stance.

annotations.anchor_quote: stream line women education
annotations.correction: streamline women's education
annotations.comment: Awkward phrasing and missing possessive 'stream line women education'; revise to 'streamline women's education'.

annotations.anchor_quote: lencew their rights
annotations.correction: know their rights
annotations.comment: Spelling error 'lencew' disrupts readability; correct to 'know their rights'.

annotations.anchor_quote: III. Impediments in the way of enlarging female mind with education
annotations.comment: Impediments section over-emphasized with 6 sub-points vs 5 in main arguments; balance by consolidating similar points.

annotations.anchor_quote: 1. Societal barriers and poverty 1 Case in point: (1) Prioritizing boy's education over girl's (2) Not having the means to support education.
annotations.comment: Sub-points merely descriptive listings; add cause-effect analysis linking poverty to perpetuating blind obedience.

annotations.anchor_quote: (2) In Pakistan 18% girls in age 12-16 get married.
annotations.comment: Statistic lacks source attribution; cite reliable source like UNICEF or Pakistan census.

annotations.anchor_quote: Case in point: Patriarch head of the family SYED TAIN decides and provides.
annotations.comment: Irrelevant personal name 'SYED TAIN'; replace with general example of patriarchal control.

annotations.anchor_quote: Females economic dependence on others to provide for their education.
annotations.correction: Females' economic dependence on others to provide for their education.
annotations.comment: Add possessive apostrophe 'Females' economic'; correct to proper grammatical form.

annotations.anchor_quote: 5. Massive digital in present age of online learning. Case in Point: Global digital divide 68%, for women 51% 1 (3) Pakistan female digital divide 24% whereas mode's is 48%
annotations.comment: Sub-points merely descriptive with stats lacking analytical depth; add cause-effect explanation of impediment impact

annotations.anchor_quote: Pakistan female digital divide 24% whereas mode's is 48%
annotations.comment: Source for factual data not mentioned; attribute to reliable source like UN or World Bank

annotations.anchor_quote: "altidunal" change rather than BY more LawsD TAIMOOR BUKHARI
annotations.correction: attitudinal change rather than by more laws
annotations.comment: Misspelling 'altidunal' and erratic capitalization; correct to 'attitudinal' and standardize phrasing

annotations.anchor_quote: from for flung areas can educate themselves.
annotations.correction: from far-flung areas can educate themselves.
annotations.comment: Misspelling 'for flung'; correct to 'far-flung' for proper term

annotations.anchor_quote: NV. Conclusion.
annotations.comment: Conclusion section marked but lacks subpoints or content, causing imbalance; add structured subpoints for summary and recommendations to balance outline.

annotations.anchor_quote: NV. Conclusion.
annotations.correction: V. Conclusion.

annotations.anchor_quote: 3. In Pakistan, ensure implementation of Article 25, and Article 38
annotations.comment: IV. point appears abrupt/incomplete at page end; connect to prior measures and transition to conclusion for better flow.

annotations.anchor_quote: It has, undeniably, wisely phrased that it needs education to break free females from blind obedience.
annotations.comment: Restates topic but lacks explicit thesis and overview of main arguments; add clear thesis stating stance and structure.

annotations.anchor_quote: Unfortunately, there are various social, economic, political and traditional hindrances in way of educating the females.
annotations.comment: Mentions impediments but no preview of arguments or solutions; include roadmap of key sections for better framing.

annotations.anchor_quote: They question and rationalize and - only than decide on whether to comply or not.
annotations.comment: Repeats 'questions, thinks, rationalizes' idea from prior sentences; consolidate to eliminate redundancy.

annotations.anchor_quote: By enlarging the femal mind is by virtue of educating them makes them better not only for their own good but also for the entire society.
annotations.correction: Enlarging the female mind through education makes them better not only for their own good but also for the entire society.
annotations.comment: Run-on with spelling error ('femal'); restructure into one clear grammatical sentence.

annotations.anchor_quote: They make more than 50% of the world population
annotations.comment: Statistic lacks cited source; attribute to reliable source like UN data.

annotations.anchor_quote: only educated females can break free from the shackles of patriarchal mindset that exists on the very principle of blind obedience.
annotations.comment: Descriptive claim lacks critical analysis; explain cause-effect of education fostering rational questioning.

annotations.anchor_quote: their cause for no more blind obedience can take up momentum. There are many ways to prove that when female mind is enlarged by education, the blind obedience ends.
annotations.comment: Repeats thesis verbatim; consolidate to eliminate redundancy and advance argument.

annotations.anchor_quote: They do not let women think once for her own self and
annotations.comment: Ends abruptly mid-sentence; complete thought with topic sentence recap or transition to next point.

annotations.anchor_quote: This mind set decides what women cando, or can not; what women can wear or wear not;
annotations.correction: This mindset decides what women can do or cannot; what women can wear or not wear;
annotations.comment: Spelling/grammar errors ('mind set', 'cando'); fix contractions and parallel structure for clarity.

annotations.anchor_quote: Secondly, when female gets - - - education, she secures economic sustenance for herself. In this way she does not depend on any one economically and can make impartial, out of free will decision for the future. This protects her from blindly following someone just because they materially provide for them According to UNFD (United Nations Population Fund) there is only 28% female labour force which means the other 72% women are economi- cally dependent on their male guardian. Hence, when the female is educated, she comes out of this vicious cycle of dependence and makes decisions for herself just as she likes. Thirdly, Females when edu- cated, are fully aware of their rights.
annotations.comment: Paragraph mixes two ideas (economic sustenance and rights); split into separate paragraphs each starting with a topic sentence.

annotations.anchor_quote: But when a female is educated, she does not comply blindly and learns to think and care for herself too in the same way. Secondly, when female gets - - - education, she secures economic sustenance for herself. In this way she does not depend on any one economically and can make impartial, out of free will decision for the future. This protects her from blindly following someone just because they materially provide for them
annotations.comment: Repeats 'blindly' twice closely; rephrase one instance (e.g., 'unquestioningly') to eliminate redundancy.

annotations.anchor_quote: Hence, when the female is educated, she comes out of this vicious cycle of dependence and makes decisions for herself just as she likes.
annotations.comment: Descriptive summary lacks analysis; add cause-effect reasoning showing how education breaks dependence to end obedience.

annotations.anchor_quote: when female gets - - - education
annotations.correction: when a female gets an education
annotations.comment: Missing articles and dashes; add 'a' before 'female' and 'an' before 'education' for proper grammar.

annotations.anchor_quote: According to UNFD (United Nations Population Fund) there is only 28% female labour force which means the other 72% women are economi- cally dependent on their male guardian.

annotations.anchor_quote: S Despite the need and significance of female education, there are some mounting impediments that paralyze the process of women education.

Analyze how child marriages and government shortcomings specifically foster blind obedience, beyond listing impacts.

Explicitly tie child marriage ban to enabling education and thesis on female empowerment.
Consolidate repeated mentions of economic independence into one analytical point.
